---
title: Booking Status Sync
description: Core sync mechanism that maps PMS booking status changes to HubSpot deal property updates.
date: 2026-03-14
tags: hubspot, pms, sync, status-mapping, deal-properties, financial, error-handling

# Booking Status Sync -- PMS to HubSpot

> Covers [S2 Sync Triggers -- PMS Status Changes](../hubspot-pms-sync-spec.md#layer-2-sync-triggers--pms-status-changes) of the HubSpot / PMS Integration Spec.

## When This Fires

Every booking status change in the PMS triggers `syncBookingToHubspot()`. This is the core sync mechanism -- it computes all financial fields, maps the PMS status to a HubSpot stage, and writes deal properties.

## Diagram

```
  +------------------------------+
  | PMS booking status changes   |
  +---------|--------------------+
            v
  +------------------------------+
  | syncBookingToHubspot() fires |
  +---------|--------------------+
            v
  +------------------------------+
  | status = blacklisted?        |
  +------|------------|----------+
     YES |            | NO
         v            v
  +-----------+  +-----------------------------------+
  | Return    |  | Fetch booking record              |
  | early --  |  +---------|-------------------------+
  | no sync   |            v
  +-----------+  +-----------------------------------+
                 | Fetch unit (if assigned)           |
                 | -> listing property                |
                 +---------|-------------------------+
                           v
                 +-----------------------------------+
                 | Query booking_addons              |
                 | -> room_revenue,                  |
                 |    total_add_on_revenue,           |
                 |    f_b_revenue, fee_revenue,       |
                 |    tax_revenue                     |
                 +---------|-------------------------+
                           v
                 +-----------------------------------+
                 | Query payments                    |
                 | -> cash_collected, refund_amount   |
                 +---------|-------------------------+
                           v
                 +-----------------------------------+
                 | Map status -> stage               |
                 | via HUBSPOT_STAGE_MAP             |
                 +---------|-------------------------+
                           v
                 +-----------------------------------+
                 | Build properties object           |
                 | (always + conditional fields)      |
                 +---------|-------------------------+
                           v
                 +-----------------------------------+
                 | hubspotDealId exists on booking?   |
                 +------|------------|---------------+
                    YES |            | NO
                        v            v
              +--------------+  +-----------------------------+
              | UPDATE deal  |  | CREATE contact (or dedup    |
              |              |  |   via 409 + email search)   |
              +----|------|--+  | CREATE deal                 |
                   |      |     |   + pipeline, dealname,     |
                   |      |     |     deal_source             |
                   |      |     | ASSOCIATE deal <-> contact  |
                   |      |     |   (v4 API, typeId: 3)       |
                   |      |     | Store hubspotDealId +       |
                   |      |     |   hubspotContactId on       |
                   |      |     |   booking record            |
                   |      |     +-------|---------------------+
                   |      |             |
                   v      v             v
              +------------------------------------+
              | Success: log hubspot_synced        |
              | Failure: log hubspot_sync_failed   |
              +------------------------------------+
              | Return { synced, dealId }          |
              +------------------------------------+
```

## Status to Stage Mapping

```
SCHEMA: Status -> Stage Mapping
================================
PMS Status         | HubSpot Stage        | Stage ID       | Pipeline
-------------------|----------------------|----------------|-------------------
inquiry            | Inquiry Made         | 1308814483     | Reservation
deposit_paid       | Promo Deposit Paid   | 1311051092     | Reservation
quoted             | Quote                | 1308814486     | Reservation
pending_approval   | Pending Approval     | 1309405752     | Reservation
confirmed          | Closed Won           | 1308814489     | Reservation
checked_in         | Closed Won           | 1308814489     | Reservation
checked_out        | Closed Won           | 1308814489     | Reservation
cancelled          | Cancelled            | 1309341806     | Reservation
nurturing          | Follow-Up Needed     | 1308812207     | Nurture
not_qualified      | (see note below)     | TBD            | TBD
rebooking_hold     | Rebooking Sign Up    | contractsent   | Rebooking Program
blacklisted        | --                   | --             | NO SYNC
```

> **`not_qualified` -- no pipeline exists.** The spec lists a "Not Qualified" pipeline, but it does not exist in the live HubSpot account. The cheat sheet references "CL-NOT QUALIFIED" as a **stage within** the Reservation, Past Guest, and Nurture pipelines (stage ID `1309341808` in Reservation, `1308811137` in Nurture). Decision needed: create a dedicated pipeline, or map `not_qualified` to the CL-Not Qualified stage within the deal's current pipeline.

> **Undocumented stage ID `1310031995`.** A sampled live Rebooking pipeline deal (ID 57749907556) has `dealstage: 1310031995`, which does not appear in the spec's Rebooking pipeline stages (`contractsent`, `998541953`, `998541954`). This stage needs identification and documentation.

> **`nurturing` label note.** The spec calls this stage "Nurturing" but the live HubSpot label is "Follow-Up Needed". This document uses the live label.

## Properties Always Written (every sync)

```
SCHEMA: Properties Always Written (every sync)
================================================
HubSpot Property                       | Computation
---------------------------------------|--------------------------------------------
dealstage                              | HUBSPOT_STAGE_MAP[booking.status].stageId
amount                                 | booking.totalAmount (see note)
gross_revenue                          | booking.totalAmount
room_revenue                           | booking.nightlyRate * booking.nights
total_add_on_revenue                   | Sum of booking_addons where category != room_revenue
f_b_revenue                            | Sum of booking_addons where category = f_and_b / food_beverage
fee_revenue                            | Sum of booking_addons where category = fee
tax_revenue                            | Sum of booking_addons.taxAmount
cash_collected                         | Sum of payments where status=succeeded, type!=refund
refund_amount                          | Sum of payments where type=refund, status=succeeded
revenue_generated_less_taxes__fees     | booking.totalAmount - sum(booking_addons.taxAmount)
quote_amount                           | booking.quoteAmount
```

> **`amount` may not be written in production.** All sampled live Reservation pipeline deals show `amount: ""` despite having populated `gross_revenue` and `cash_collected`. This suggests the PMS may not currently be writing `amount`, or it is being cleared by a HubSpot automation. Needs investigation.

## Properties Conditionally Written

```
SCHEMA: Properties Conditionally Written
==========================================
HubSpot Property      | Condition
----------------------|------------------------------------------------------
checkin_date          | When booking.checkIn is set
checkout_date         | When booking.checkOut is set
confirmation_number   | When booking.confirmationNumber is set
listing               | When unit is assigned: "{unit.code} - {unit.name}"
hubspot_owner_id      | When booking.hubspotOwnerId is set (sales-rep deals)
quote_expiry_date     | When booking.quoteExpiresAt is set
last_stay_date        | On checked_out status -- set to booking.checkOut
```

> **Conditional vs always-written.** The spec Summary lists `checkin_date`, `checkout_date`, `confirmation_number`, `listing`, `hubspot_owner_id`, and `quote_expiry_date` under "On Every Sync." Practically these are included in the properties object on every call but may carry null/empty values when not set. The distinction is implementation-specific. This document lists them as conditional to reflect that they carry meaningful data only when the booking has the corresponding value.

## Properties on Deal Creation Only

```
SCHEMA: Properties on Deal Creation Only
==========================================
HubSpot Property  | Value                                        | Notes
------------------|----------------------------------------------|-------------------------------
pipeline          | PIPELINE_IDS[pipeline]                       | HubSpot handles subsequent
                  |   Reservation: 873502775                     |   pipeline switching via
                  |   Nurture:     873061385                     |   workflow automations
                  |   Rebooking:   873057543                     |
------------------|----------------------------------------------|-------------------------------
dealname          | "{guestName} -- {confirmationNumber}"        |
------------------|----------------------------------------------|-------------------------------
deal_source       | "online_booking" (guest self-serve)           | NOTE: "online_booking" is NOT
                  | Staff selection (sales-rep bookings)          |   currently a valid enum value
                  |                                              |   in HubSpot deal_source.
                  |                                              |   Must add it or use existing.
```

> **UTM properties do NOT exist on deals.** `utm_source`, `utm_medium`, `utm_campaign` exist only as contact-level analytics properties via HubSpot tracking. If UTM data needs to live on deals, custom deal properties must be created first. Otherwise, UTM writes must be removed from deal creation code.

## Update vs Create Logic

```
if (booking.hubspotDealId) {
    -> UPDATE existing deal with properties
       (always-written + conditional properties)
} else {
    -> CREATE or FIND HubSpot contact
       - Create contact with guestEmail, firstname, lastname, phone
       - On 409 conflict: search by email, use existing contact ID
       - PMS never creates duplicate contacts

    -> CREATE new deal
       - Set pipeline (creation-only -- HubSpot handles switching)
       - Set dealname: "{guestName} -- {confirmationNumber}"
       - Set deal_source: "online_booking" (guest self-serve)
                          or staff selection (sales-rep)
       - Set all always-written + conditional properties

    -> ASSOCIATE deal <-> contact
       - v4 associations API, typeId: 3

    -> STORE on booking record
       - hubspotDealId  (deal record ID)
       - hubspotContactId  (contact record ID)
}
```

## Error Handling

- All sync operations are **fire-and-forget** -- never block the booking status change.
- On success: `logEvent()` with type `hubspot_synced`.
- On failure: `logEvent()` with type `hubspot_sync_failed`, includes error message and attempted properties.
- Recovery: manual re-sync via AI tool `syncBookingToHubspot`.
- Future: event-driven architecture (QStash) provides automatic retries.

Specific error scenarios from the spec:

| Scenario | Behavior |
|---|---|
| HubSpot API timeout | QStash retries (3x, exponential backoff) |
| HubSpot rate limit (429) | QStash retries with backoff |
| Invalid deal ID | Log to events_log, alert -- may need manual intervention |
| Contact 409 conflict | Search by email, use existing contact (handled in create path) |
| Missing `HUBSPOT_ACCESS_TOKEN` (PMS) | Throw on startup -- PMS requires HubSpot |
| Missing `HUBSPOT_ACCESS_TOKEN` (web) | Graceful degradation -- web works without HubSpot |

## Downstream HubSpot Automations

The PMS writes deal properties -- HubSpot workflow automations react to those writes. Key automations triggered by this sync:

| PMS Status Change | HubSpot Automation |
|---|---|
| `confirmed` (Closed Won) | Creates Upsell deal, creates Rebooking deal, 265-day anniversary task, lifecycle -> Mountain Customer |
| `cancelled` | Moves deal to Recover pipeline, tasks original deal owner |
| `nurturing` | Creates new deal in Nurturing pipeline |
| `checked_out` | Past Guest pipeline timing starts (requires `last_stay_date`) |
| `not_qualified` | Lifecycle -> Mountain Not Qualified |
